title: 3D-Aware VLMs with Implicit and Explicit Geometries
headline: Enhancing Vision-Language Models with 3D Geometry via Implicit and Explicit Representations
tldr: VLM-IE3D augments vision-language models with learned 3D geometries from RGB videos, enabling fine-grained spatial reasoning. The framework integrates both implicit and explicit representations to bridge the gap between 2D inputs and 3D tasks.
significance: By fusing implicit and explicit 3D geometry, VLM-IE3D empowers VLMs to perform spatial reasoning tasks that were previously limited to 2D inputs, opening new possibilities in robotics, AR, and autonomous navigation.

title: Streaming Multi-Agent Autoregressive Diffusion Model with World State Registers
headline: WorldWeaver: Streaming Multi-Agent Diffusion Model with Persistent World State Registers
tldr: WorldWeaver introduces a streaming autoregressive diffusion framework that maintains shared world state across multiple agents and viewpoints. By integrating state registers, it enables consistent, evolving observations in multi-agent interactive environments.
significance: This approach resolves the state consistency challenge in multi-agent video generation, enabling more realistic and coordinated simulations.

title: Unified Video Dense Prediction from Disjoint Data
headline: Unified Video Dense Prediction from Disjoint Data: Introducing the UniD Framework
tldr: We present UniD, a framework that enables dense prediction across geometry, appearance, and semantics using disjoint datasets without requiring full co-annotation or expensive pseudo-labeling. UniD leverages cross-task consistency and efficient training to unify disparate annotations for video scene understanding.
significance: UniD bridges fragmented annotations, enabling scalable, multi-task video perception without costly data alignment.

title: Inference-Time Scaling of Diffusion Models via Progressive Seed Pruning
headline: Speeding Diffusion Models at Inference with Progressive Seed Pruning for High-Quality Generation
tldr: This paper introduces a method to prune seed noise in diffusion models during inference, reducing compute while maintaining image quality. By progressively discarding low-impact seed components, the approach achieves near real‑time performance without increasing memory usage.
significance: It bridges the gap between diffusion model quality and practical deployment speed, enabling real‑time conditional image generation.

title: AXIS: A Growable Community-Driven Data Engine for Scalable Robot Manipulation
headline: AXIS: Community-Driven, Scalable Data Engine Enabling Browser-Based Teleoperation for Robot Manipulation
tldr: AXIS is a growable, community-driven data engine that facilitates large-scale robot manipulation learning by enabling browser-based teleoperation. It provides an open-source benchmark and scalable pipeline that overcomes hardware and operator bottlenecks.
significance: AXIS democratizes robot learning by making high-quality demonstration data accessible and scalable, accelerating research and deployment.

title: XMM-Newton and Swift Unveil Another X-Ray Transient in NGC 4945, XMM J130514.64-493311.27
headline: XMM-Newton and Swift Spot New X-Ray Transient in NGC 4945
tldr: Using XMM-Newton and Swift observations, astronomers discovered a new X-ray transient in the nearby galaxy NGC 4945. The transient adds to the growing list of variable high-energy sources in this starburst galaxy, offering insights into compact object populations.
significance: This discovery expands our understanding of the dynamic X-ray source population in NGC 4945 and highlights the importance of coordinated multi-mission monitoring.

title: Flow-based Phase-space Tomography of Continuous-variable Quantum States
headline: Flow-Based Phase-Space Tomography of Continuous-Variable Quantum States Using Neural Density Models
tldr: We present QST-Flow, a flow-based generative framework that reconstructs continuous-variable quantum states by modeling phase-space quasiprobabilities with neural densities, avoiding truncated density matrices. This approach reduces computational cost while preserving non-Gaussian features, enabling scalable tomography of high-dimensional quantum systems.
significance: QST-Flow offers a scalable, efficient alternative to traditional tomography, potentially accelerating quantum computing and communication research.

title: Scale Up Strategically: Learning Compositional Generalization via Bias-Aware Evaluation and Data Collection for Robotic Manipulation
headline: Diagnosing and Fixing Shortcut Bias in Robot Language Understanding
tldr: We present a bias‑aware evaluation framework that pinpoints which instruction components (color, verb, object, size, spatial relations) cause pretrained policies to rely on shortcuts. By collecting targeted data, we improve compositional generalization for robotic manipulation tasks.
significance: The work offers a systematic method to detect and mitigate shortcut learning, enabling robots to reliably interpret diverse natural language commands.

title: Symmetry and Quantum Geometry in Bloch Bands
headline: Exploring Symmetry Constraints on Quantum Geometric Quantities in Bloch Bands
tldr: The paper investigates how lattice symmetries limit the variance of Berry curvature and the integrated quantum metric in tight‑binding models. It shows that certain symmetry classes enforce strict bounds on these geometric measures, impacting electronic properties.
significance: Understanding symmetry‑imposed limits on quantum geometry is crucial for designing materials with tailored electronic responses.

title: GraphVid: Interactive Graph-Controllable Video Generation
headline: GraphVid: Graph-Based Control for Interactive Multi-Object Video Generation Using Text and Motion Inputs
tldr: GraphVid introduces a graph-based interface that lets users specify multi-object interactions in videos, overcoming limitations of pixel-level motion controls. By integrating trajectory graphs with diffusion models, it scales to complex scenes and handles occlusion without requiring precise tracks.
significance: It enables scalable, intuitive control over complex video dynamics, advancing practical video generation.

title: Quantum Fisher Information in semiclassical magnon systems
headline: Quantum Fisher Information Reveals Entanglement in Semiclassical Magnon Spin Systems
tldr: This study uses finite-size spin models to connect QFI with entanglement metrics in solid-state systems. It demonstrates how QFI can serve as a spectroscopic probe for many-body quantum correlations.
significance: By clarifying the role of QFI in condensed matter, the work provides a new diagnostic tool for detecting quantum entanglement in realistic materials.

title: Primordial black holes forming during kination: the trapped, the overdense, and the void
headline: Primordial Black Holes Formed During Kination: Trapped, Overdense, and Void Regions
tldr: The study investigates primordial black hole formation in a single-field model that transitions from slow roll through an ultra-slow-roll phase into kination. Using nonlinear numerical-relativity simulations, the authors track sub-Hubble field fluctuations across the transition to determine the resulting super-Hubble curvature profiles and their evolution.
significance: This work delivers the first detailed simulation-based mapping of how kination influences primordial black hole abundance and spatial distribution, refining constraints on early-universe dynamics.

title: The sharp exponent for the minimal distance problem
headline: Sharp Exponent Achieved for Minimal Distance Problem in Plane Geometry
tldr: The paper proves that for any ε>0, one can construct arbitrarily large families of point-line pairs in the unit square with pairwise distances at least n^{-2/3-ε}. This resolves the minimal distance problem by matching the known upper bound up to an arbitrarily small exponent.
significance: It settles a long-standing open question about the optimal exponent governing the minimal distance between points and lines in the plane.

title: Interaction-model dependence in calorimetric energy reconstruction methods due to non-linear material effects in modern neutrino detectors
headline: Non-linear Detector Effects Challenge Calorimetric Energy Reconstruction in Modern Neutrino Experiments
tldr: Calorimetric energy reconstruction in LAr‑TPCs and scintillator detectors is hampered by non‑linear response effects such as Birks quenching and recombination. These ambiguities degrade the precision required for neutrino oscillation measurements.
significance: Accurate energy reconstruction is essential for precise neutrino oscillation parameters, and understanding detector non‑linearities is critical for future experiments.

title: ATSplat: Compact Feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Adaptive Token Expansion
headline: ATSplat: Efficient Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Adaptive Token Expansion
tldr: ATSplat introduces a compact, feed-forward 3D Gaussian splatting framework that expands tokens adaptively, preserving scene-adaptive capacity while enabling real-time novel-view synthesis. The method regresses Gaussians directly from pixels and lifts them along camera rays, achieving high quality with fewer primitives.
significance: This approach bridges the gap between high-quality 3DGS and efficient real-time rendering, making advanced novel-view synthesis accessible for interactive applications.

title: Pinned nonempty interior and volumes of simplices
headline: Pinned Nonempty Interior Problems and Volume Estimates for Simplices via Radon Transforms
tldr: We analyze pinned nonempty-interior configurations and simplex volume problems using smooth scalar maps whose localized Radon transforms are nondegenerate Fourier integral operators. The work extends Greenleaf, Iosevich, and collaborators’ calculations to higher dimensions, yielding new bounds on interior measures and simplex volumes.
significance: This study provides sharper geometric estimates for Radon transform-based problems, advancing our understanding of high-dimensional volume distribution.

title: Flux-Corrected Diagonal Frog: second order and positivity at all time steps
headline: Second-Order Flux-Corrected Diagonal Frog Preserves Positivity at All Time Steps
tldr: This paper introduces a nonlinear extension of the Diagonal Frog scheme that maintains second-order accuracy while guaranteeing positivity for the Fokker-Planck equation at any time step. By splitting the second-order directional operator, the method overcomes the strict time-step limitation of previous linear approaches.
significance: It resolves a long-standing barrier in numerical PDEs, enabling robust, positivity-preserving simulations of diffusion processes without restrictive time-step constraints.

title: Mapped ADMM: A Robust Algorithm for 1-Bit mMIMO Detection
headline: Mapped ADMM Enhances 1‑Bit mMIMO Detection
tldr: The paper shows that one‑bit massive MIMO detection can be cast as a binary classification problem solvable by SVM. By decentralizing the SVM into multiple classifiers and applying consensus ADMM, the authors achieve a robust, efficient detection algorithm.
significance: This approach offers a scalable, low‑complexity solution for next‑generation wireless systems requiring ultra‑low‑precision hardware.

title: The road towards precision measurements of $H_0$ with bright sirens in the Einstein Telescope era
headline: Precision H0 Measurements with Bright Sirens in the Einstein Telescope Era
tldr: Gravitational-wave standard sirens offer a direct probe of cosmic expansion. This study forecasts how bright siren catalogs from the Einstein Telescope will tighten constraints on the Hubble constant.
significance: It demonstrates that next-generation detectors can achieve sub-percent precision on H0, resolving current tensions.

title: Fermionic pairs, from the surface to the bulk
headline: From Surface to Bulk: Understanding Fermionic Pairing Across Finite and Extended Systems
tldr: Fermionic pairing drives quantum phenomena in both extended systems like ultracold gases and finite systems such as nuclei and quantum dots. While the BCS–BEC crossover describes pair size evolution in large systems, finite systems exhibit distinct pairing behavior due to confinement.
significance: Understanding how pairing manifests across scales informs both fundamental physics and potential applications in quantum technologies.

title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values
headline: LKValues: Aligning LLMs with Sri Lankan Cultural Values in Sinhala
tldr: This study introduces LKValues, a benchmark for evaluating LLM alignment with Sri Lankan societal norms in Sinhala. It highlights cultural gaps in current models and proposes fine-tuning strategies for more inclusive AI.
significance: By addressing Sinhala-specific values, LKValues paves the way for culturally sensitive AI deployment in multilingual societies.

title: Efficiently Simulable Pauli Correlation Encoding
headline: Reducing Measurement Overhead in Pauli Correlation Encoding for Binary Optimization Using Efficient Simulation
tldr: Pauli Correlation Encoding maps binary variables to Pauli observables but requires many measurements, limiting scalability. This study presents a simulation-based strategy that drastically cuts the number of expectation values needed, accelerating quantum optimization workflows.
significance: By lowering measurement costs, the approach brings practical quantum binary optimization closer to real-world deployment.

title: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
headline: Reducing Repetitive Copying in Long-Context LLM Reasoning with Evidence-Aware RL
tldr: Large language models often copy input text instead of generating meaningful reasoning in long-context tasks. This paper introduces evidence-aware reinforcement learning to reduce copying and improve reasoning quality.
significance: It advances reliable reasoning in LLMs, enabling more trustworthy solutions for complex, context-heavy problems.

title: Appearance Pointers -- Multimodal Region Control of Diffusion Transformers
headline: Multimodal Region Control for Diffusion Transformers: Enhancing Precise Image Generation
tldr: This work introduces a method to give users fine-grained regional control over Diffusion Transformers, enabling precise manipulation of materials, object identities, and spatial layouts. By integrating heterogeneous tokens and spatial guidance, the approach surpasses text-only prompting and opens new possibilities for creative professionals.
significance: It bridges the gap between text-based prompts and spatially-aware image editing, empowering designers with unprecedented control.

title: Masked Visual Actions for Unified World Modeling
headline: Masked Visual Actions: Bridging Video Models and Physical Manipulation for Unified World Modeling
tldr: This paper introduces Masked Visual Actions, a method that encodes robotic actions directly into the visual space of pretrained video models, enabling them to predict and plan interactions in a unified representation. By aligning action signals with learned visual priors, the approach facilitates more natural and efficient world modeling for robotics.
significance: It enables seamless integration of visual perception and action planning in robotics, leveraging foundation video models.

title: ExpertVerse: A General-Purpose Benchmark for Expert-Level Reasoning in Knowledge-Intensive Visual Synthesis
headline: ExpertVerse: Benchmarking Expert-Level Reasoning in Knowledge-Driven Visual Generation for Multimodal Models and Generative AI
tldr: ExpertVerse introduces a benchmark that evaluates multimodal generative models on expert-level, knowledge-intensive visual reasoning tasks, moving beyond simple commonsense or direct knowledge recall. It tests models’ ability to perform deep causal reasoning and knowledge-driven image synthesis, highlighting gaps in current state-of-the-art systems.
significance: By providing a rigorous, capability-centric evaluation, ExpertVerse pushes the field toward truly intelligent visual synthesis that can reason with complex knowledge.

title: Integral representation of the neutrino mass-squared differences
headline: Integral Representation of Neutrino Mass-Squared Differences: A New Theoretical Perspective
tldr: We propose a simple integral representation for neutrino mass-squared differences, offering a complementary view on oscillation parameters. This approach may help constrain theoretical models and guide future experiments.
significance: Providing a novel analytic framework could sharpen predictions for neutrino oscillation experiments and aid in determining the absolute neutrino mass scale.

title: OmniReasoner: Thinking with Long Audio-Video via Native Tool Use
headline: OmniReasoner: Enabling Long Audio‑Video Reasoning via Tool‑Use in Omnimodal LLMs
tldr: OmniReasoner trains LLMs to selectively invoke tools for long audio‑video inputs, reducing the need for high‑fidelity storage. The framework combines supervised fine‑tuning and reinforcement learning to identify when and where tool calls are most beneficial.
significance: By allowing LLMs to focus computational resources on decisive evidence, OmniReasoner improves efficiency and accuracy in multimodal reasoning tasks.

title: CodeRescue: Budget-Calibrated Recovery Routing for Coding Agents
headline: CodeRescue: Budget‑Aware Recovery Routing for Coding Agents Using Execution Feedback
tldr: CodeRescue introduces a budget‑calibrated routing framework that uses execution feedback to guide cheap‑model retries before escalating to expensive models. The approach reduces cost while maintaining high success rates in coding tasks.
significance: By leveraging actionable execution feedback, CodeRescue improves cost efficiency and reliability of large language models in code generation.

title: Agents in the Wild: Where Research Meets Deployment
headline: From Lab to Live: Deploying Agentic LLM Systems in Real-World Applications
tldr: Agentic systems built on large language models are moving beyond research prototypes to production deployments across software engineering, science, and finance. This shift highlights the need to move beyond benchmarks toward real-world integration and operational reliability.
significance: The transition underscores the importance of practical deployment considerations, such as scalability, robustness, and tool coordination, for the broader adoption of AI agents.

title: 1-Lipschitz Neural Networks on Hadamard Manifolds
headline: Building 1‑Lipschitz Neural Networks on Hadamard Manifolds for Robust Geometric Learning
tldr: We introduce gradient‑descent layers that are 1‑Lipschitz and quasi‑α‑firmly nonexpansive, enabling neural networks to operate on Hadamard manifolds. This framework extends Lipschitz‑constrained learning to non‑Euclidean spaces, improving robustness and stability.
significance: This work generalizes Lipschitz‑constrained neural networks to curved spaces, opening new avenues for robust geometric deep learning.

title: Fundamental limits of distributed multiclass classification from simple binary decisions
headline: Distributed Multiclass Classification: Limits of Logarithmic Binary Decision Aggregation in Networks
tldr: The paper analyzes how a K-class classifier built from only O(log K) simple binary hyperplane classifiers behaves in distributed settings. It establishes theoretical bounds on accuracy and shows that even with optimal binary decisions, the overall performance cannot surpass a certain threshold, guiding practical design of scalable multi‑class systems.
significance: It provides the first rigorous limits for distributed multiclass learning, informing the design of efficient, scalable classifiers in edge and networked AI.

title: Provable diffusion-based posterior sampling for linear inverse problems via DDIM
headline: Provable Diffusion-Based Posterior Sampling for Linear Inverse Problems via DDIM
tldr: We introduce PDDIM, a diffusion-based posterior sampler that provably solves linear inverse problems efficiently. The algorithm achieves rigorous guarantees while reducing computational overhead compared to existing methods.
significance: PDDIM bridges the gap between theoretical rigor and practical efficiency in diffusion-based inverse problem solving.

title: ROMS-IMLE: A Minimalist Approach to Competitive Single-Step Generative Modelling
headline: ROMS-IMLE: Simplifying Single-Step Generative Models for Competitive Performance in Real-Time Applications
tldr: ROMS-IMLE introduces a minimalist framework that achieves competitive results with single-step generative models, reducing complexity compared to multi-step diffusion or flow matching approaches. The method demonstrates comparable performance while enabling faster inference and easier deployment.
significance: It shows that simpler generative architectures can rival state-of-the-art diffusion models, potentially lowering barriers to adoption.

title: ISO: An RLVR-Native Optimization Stack
headline: ISO: Spectral Inheritance in RLVR for Efficient Weight Updates in LLMs
tldr: We introduce ISO, a reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR)-native optimization stack that leverages spectral inheritance to convert reward signals into efficient weight-space updates. Our analysis demonstrates that spectral structure of model weights can be exploited to accelerate training while preserving reasoning capabilities.
significance: ISO provides a principled framework to bridge reward signals and weight updates, potentially transforming how foundation models learn from verifiable feedback.

title: The Many Senses of Visual Similarity: A Text-Prompted Image Perceptual Metric
headline: Contextual Visual Similarity: Text-Prompted Perceptual Metric for Human-Driven Dataset
tldr: We present a dataset of human similarity judgments on image triplets that captures context-dependent aspects like shape and color. Our text-prompted metric learns to condition similarity on specific attributes, offering a nuanced alternative to scalar metrics.
significance: This work enables more precise perceptual modeling by allowing similarity to be tailored to desired visual attributes.

title: Patch Policy: Efficient Embodied Control via Dense Visual Representations
headline: Vision Transformers Enable Efficient, Fine‑Grained Robot Control with Dense Visual Representations
tldr: The authors propose a patch policy that directly uses dense visual embeddings from pretrained Vision Transformers, avoiding costly global token compression. This approach retains fine‑grained spatial information and achieves efficient, real‑time robot control.
significance: It demonstrates that large‑scale visual pretraining can be harnessed for embodied tasks without sacrificing spatial detail, improving policy efficiency.

title: Automated Discovery Has No Universally Superior Harness
headline: No One-Size-Fits-All: Automated Discovery Systems Lack a Superior Universal Harness
tldr: Autonomous discovery platforms like OpenEvolve and TTT-Discover bundle multiple design choices into single recipes. Due to high cost and stochasticity, current harnesses are rarely compared comprehensively, underscoring the need for modular, benchmark-driven evaluation.
significance: This work reveals that the absence of a universally superior harness hampers reproducibility and progress in automated discovery research.

title: Cosmological consequences of a dynamical dark matter in the light of DESI DR2 measurements
headline: DESI DR2 Supports Dynamical Dark Matter Over Phantom Dark Energy
tldr: DESI DR2 data shows a preference for a null energy condition violating dynamical dark energy, but this study proposes that the signal could instead arise from unconventional dark matter dynamics. A dynamical dark matter model with a smoothly interpolating equation of state provides an alternative explanation for the observed cosmological signatures.
significance: This work challenges the prevailing interpretation of DESI data, suggesting that dark matter dynamics may play a pivotal role in shaping cosmic acceleration.

title: Theoretical uncertainties in reconstructing model parameters with gravitational waves from supercooled phase transitions
headline: Uncertainties in Gravitational-Wave Reconstruction of Supercooled Phase Transition Models for Future Detectors
tldr: Future gravitational-wave detectors could observe signals from cosmological first-order phase transitions. This study quantifies theoretical uncertainties in mapping such signals back to particle-physics parameters in classically scale-invariant models.
significance: Understanding these uncertainties is crucial for interpreting future GW observations as probes of beyond-Standard-Model physics.

title: It's Not What You Say, It's How You Say It: Evaluating LLM Responses to Expressions of Belief
headline: Evaluating How LLMs Handle Users’ Expressions of Belief
tldr: The study examines how large language models respond to varied linguistic expressions of belief, determining when they should accept user-provided context versus relying on prior knowledge. It highlights the impact of linguistic cues such as presuppositions, evidential markers, and tone on model behavior.
significance: Understanding LLMs’ handling of belief expressions informs safer, more context-aware AI interactions.

title: Simple Domain Generalization for Strong Pixel-Level Image Tampering Detection in Modern VLMs
headline: Domain Generalization Boosts Pixel-Level Tampering Detection in Modern Vision‑Language Models
tldr: This study introduces a simple domain generalization technique that enhances pixel‑level tampering detection across various VLMs, including ChatGPT, Gemini, and Qwen‑Image. The method demonstrates robust performance under cross‑model and out‑of‑distribution shifts, offering a scalable solution for secure image analysis.
significance: It provides a practical approach to improve the reliability of VLMs in detecting subtle image manipulations, addressing a critical security gap.

title: Logical Judgments Under Pressure: Diagnosing Syllogistic Stability with Learned Soft Prefixes
headline: Soft Prefixes Probe Syllogistic Reasoning in Large Language Models and Benchmarking
tldr: We prepend opaque soft prefixes to a syllogistic reasoning benchmark to see how learned context affects logical judgments. The study reveals which prefixes improve accuracy and how they influence model behavior across logical variations.
significance: This work uncovers how contextual embeddings can steer reasoning in LLMs, informing better prompt design.

title: FlowMimic: Mask-free Visual Editing and Generation with Pixel-pair Warped Flow Field for Online Video Editing Data Generation and Modality Mimicry
headline: FlowMimic: Mask-Free Visual Editing and Generation via Pixel-Pair Warped Flow for Online Video
tldr: FlowMimic introduces a single model that simultaneously edits and generates video and image content without requiring object masks. By leveraging pixel-pair warped flow fields, it streamlines data generation for online video editing and enables modality mimicry, reducing labor-intensive annotation.
significance: This approach eliminates the need for costly mask annotations, accelerating the creation of high-quality, editable video datasets and advancing real-time visual content manipulation.

title: Causal Discovery on Irregular Time Series
headline: Discovering Causality in Irregularly Sampled Time Series Streams Using Extended PCM
tldr: This paper extends PCM to handle irregularly sampled time series, enabling causal discovery in real-world streams like healthcare and finance. The method outperforms traditional lag-based approaches on irregular data and offers real-time applicability.
significance: It bridges the gap between causal inference and irregular time series, broadening applicability to many domains.

title: Vector Search As Nearest Neighbor Matching: RAG-based Policy Learning in Causal Inference
headline: Vector Search for Policy Learning: RAG-Based Nearest Neighbor Matching in Causal Inference
tldr: This paper introduces one- and two-step methods that use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for policy learning under the potential outcome framework. By performing vector search to retrieve action-specific evidence and then estimating conditional outcomes with a generator, the approach selects actions via a plug‑in rule.
significance: It demonstrates how vector search and LLMs can be combined to improve causal policy estimation, offering a scalable alternative to traditional methods.

title: HyperIso: A general BSM calculator for flavour observables
headline: HyperIso: Standalone Modular C++ Tool for BSM Flavour Physics Calculations
tldr: HyperIso is a new standalone program that evaluates flavour physics observables in both SM and BSM scenarios. Built on SuperIso heritage, it offers a modern C++ core with modular design and multiple user interfaces.
significance: It provides researchers with a flexible, open-source tool to efficiently compute flavour observables across diverse BSM models.

title: Knowing the Self, Understanding the World: A Dual-Cognition Benchmark for UAV Spatio-temporal Reasoning with MLLMs
headline: Dual-Cognition UAV Benchmark for Advanced Aerial Spatio-Temporal Reasoning with MLLMs
tldr: We introduce a benchmark that jointly evaluates UAV-based multimodal LLMs on self-awareness and world understanding tasks. The dataset covers diverse aerial scenarios, enabling comprehensive spatio-temporal reasoning evaluation.
significance: This benchmark bridges a gap in UAV AI by testing dual-cognition capabilities of MLLMs, fostering more robust aerial intelligence.

title: MotionForesight: Re-purposing Video Models for Future 3D Scene-Flow Prediction
headline: Predicting Future 3D Scene Flow from Monocular Human-Object Interaction Videos
tldr: MotionForesight repurposes existing video models to anticipate future 3D scene flow in human-object interactions. By analyzing short observed video clips, it predicts how objects will move, enabling more realistic physical reasoning.
significance: This approach provides a scalable way to infer physical dynamics from ordinary videos, advancing autonomous understanding of object motion.

title: Cosmological Evidence for Dark Axion-Dark Baryon Interactions from Apparent Phantom Crossing
headline: Dark Axion–Dark Baryon Interaction Mimics Phantom Crossing in Cosmic Expansion
tldr: Recent DESI BAO, CMB, and SNe Ia data suggest an apparent phantom-crossing behavior that can be explained by interactions between dark axions and dark baryons. This model provides a natural explanation for the observed expansion history without invoking exotic dark energy.
significance: It offers a testable framework linking dark matter interactions to cosmological observations, potentially resolving tensions in the Hubble constant.

title: Searching Videos as Trees: Self-Correcting Agents for Grounded Long Video QA
headline: Self-Correcting Agents for Grounded Long-Video Question Answering via Tree-Structured Exploration
tldr: The paper proposes a self-correcting agent that treats long-video QA as a tree search, enabling fine-to-coarse backtracking to refine evidence intervals. This approach improves localization accuracy and reduces search time compared to prior coarse-to-fine methods.
significance: It introduces a novel backtracking primitive that significantly enhances evidence localization in long-video QA.

title: PagedWeight: Efficient MoE LLM Serving with Dynamic Quality-Aware Weight Quantization
headline: PagedWeight: Dynamic Quantization for Efficient MoE LLM Serving, Reducing GPU Memory Footprint
tldr: PagedWeight dynamically quantizes MoE model weights during serving to balance GPU memory usage between weights and KV cache, enabling efficient inference in memory-constrained environments. The method improves throughput while maintaining accuracy, making MoE LLMs more practical for real-time applications.
significance: By decoupling weight storage from KV cache, PagedWeight allows MoE LLMs to scale to larger contexts without exhausting GPU memory, a key bottleneck in deployment.

title: A Blueprint for Equilibrium-Based Differentiable Continuous-Variable Thermodynamic Computing
headline: Energy-Efficient Thermodynamic Computing Stack Using Langevin Dynamics for Fast ML Workloads
tldr: We propose a thermodynamic computing stack that harnesses Langevin dynamics for energy-efficient, fast analog processing. This approach offers a scalable, differentiable framework for machine learning workloads with reduced energy and latency.
significance: This blueprint demonstrates how physical stochastic processes can replace conventional digital computations, potentially transforming the energy profile of AI systems.

title: Vision-Language Assistant for Emotional Reactions to Risky Driving
headline: Vision‑Language Assistant Generates Emotional Alerts for Risky Driving to Enhance Driver Safety
tldr: The study presents KYA, a vision‑language pipeline that identifies risky driving maneuvers and produces emotionally resonant feedback to improve driver awareness. By incorporating affective responses, KYA offers a more user‑centric safety experience than conventional detection systems.
significance: Integrating emotional feedback into driving risk detection bridges the gap between technical safety alerts and human drivers’ emotional states, potentially reducing accidents and enhancing user comfort.

title: A Dynamical Phase-Field Model for the Optical Properties of Ferroelectrics
headline: Dynamic Phase-Field Model Unveils Optical Behavior of Ferroelectric Photonic Devices
tldr: Researchers present a dynamic phase-field model that links ferroelectric domain evolution to optical response, enabling predictive design of photonic devices. The model captures strong coupling between spontaneous polarization and refractive index, offering a new tool for engineering ferroelectric-based optics.
significance: This work provides the first theoretical framework to predict how domain dynamics affect optical properties, accelerating the design of tunable photonic devices.

title: Cluster-Aware Matching via Laplacian Optimal Transport
headline: Cluster-Aware Matching via Laplacian Optimal Transport: Robust Region Alignment for Structured Point Clouds
tldr: This paper introduces a Laplacian optimal transport framework that aligns point clouds by matching their underlying cluster structures rather than individual points. The method achieves robust region-to-region correspondence, improving performance on datasets with intrinsic cluster organization.
significance: It provides a scalable, cluster-aware matching technique that enhances alignment accuracy for complex point cloud data, benefiting applications in remote sensing and 3D reconstruction.

title: Physics-enhanced reinforcement learning for real-time optimal control of dynamical systems
headline: Physics-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning Enables Real-Time Optimal Control of Complex Dynamical Systems
tldr: This paper proposes integrating physics models into reinforcement learning to improve sample efficiency and enable real-time control of nonlinear dynamical systems. By leveraging domain knowledge, the method reduces the need for extensive environment interactions and achieves near-optimal performance with limited sensors and actuators.
significance: It bridges the gap between data-driven RL and physics-based control, making RL viable for safety-critical real-time applications.

title: Quantum scanning synthetic optical holography
headline: Quantum Scanning Synthetic Optical Holography Enhances Undetected-Light Imaging for High-Resolution Phase Mapping
tldr: This study extends synthetic optical holography into the quantum regime, enabling quantitative phase imaging with undetected light. By integrating a controlled synthetic phase carrier into a scanning quantum imaging system, the authors achieve high-resolution, real-time holographic reconstruction.
significance: It opens new avenues for quantum-enhanced imaging techniques with potential applications in microscopy and beyond.

title: Dynamic Entanglement Distribution for Multi-User and Multi-Protocol Quantum Networking
headline: Dynamic Entanglement Distribution Enables Metropolitan-Scale Quantum Networking
tldr: The study demonstrates a quantum reconfigurable optical add-drop multiplexer (q-ROADM) that dynamically routes polarization-entangled photon pairs to six users across a campus. This approach showcases scalable, multi-user quantum communication over existing fiber infrastructure.
significance: It provides a practical, reconfigurable solution for expanding quantum networks to real-world metropolitan settings.

title: Relativistic time-commutative dynamics with $κ$-plane noncommutativity
headline: κ-Plane Noncommutativity in Relativistic Dynamics
tldr: The paper investigates time‑commutative dynamics on a κ‑deformed plane, deriving modified relativistic equations and exploring their implications for quantum‑gravity phenomenology.
significance: It provides a novel framework connecting noncommutative geometry to potentially observable relativistic effects.
